text:
Market today: The mood is tense with geopolitical risk from Trump's Iran comments, driving oil prices higher. This creates uncertainty and could weigh on growth-oriented tech and chip names, which are sensitive to broader market sentiment and economic stability.

Your holdings: SOXL (-34.27%) and SPAL (-23.62%) are leveraged ETFs deep in the red; SPAL is a new, very volatile position. Margin amplifies these drops, making them extra painful. QQQI (-2.69%) is also nearing its stop. PTNM (-15.25%) is a tiny spec with significant loss. These need attention.

Watch: Keep a close eye on oil prices and any further geopolitical developments. Also, watch for broader market reactions to profit-taking in the AI chip sector.

Bottom line: Geopolitical risk and sector-specific profit-taking suggest a cautious stance, especially with your leveraged and deep-red positions.
plays: Consider trimming or exiting SOXL given its deep loss (-34.27%) and leveraged decay. Margin amplifies this risk significantly.
Watch QQQI closely; if it hits its stop at 52, consider trimming to manage risk.
No new buys today - hold and watch. Geopolitical uncertainty and profit-taking in chips suggest caution.
